prose: Fukui reactivity indices were computed for nitrobenzene (14 atoms: 2 O, 1 N, 6 C, 5 H) at the DFT level using the B3LYP functional with the def2-TZVP basis set (chemkit tier 'standard'), in gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, via PySCF (RKS). The N−1 cation state used charge +1, multiplicity 2; the N+1 anion state used charge −1, multiplicity 2. Partial charges were computed with the Mulliken scheme. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). The geometry was taken as-supplied from the provided .xyz file (no relaxation). All SCF calculations converged; Fukui charge sums Σf⁺ = Σf⁻ = 1.0000; integrity status trustworthy=true, all checks passed. No warnings were reported. The headline value reported (0.49641782) is the maximum f⁺ index, located at atom 4 (C, the ipso carbon bonded to the nitro group), marking it as the most electrophilic site. Per-atom Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, dual descriptor) for all 14 atoms are reported in the detailed table above. Output files: nitrobenzene_fukui_dft.json and a bar chart PNG.
